description: Thin material made from pulp, used for writing or printing; the surface that receives marks. 'Pelua' names what waits empty—potential before the first word.
sound_symbolism: The word presents receptive. 'Pe' presents with flat surface, sheet ready. 'Lu' lies thin and level. Then 'a' opens to receive, paper waiting.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo pelua' (the paper). Receives marks from 'phari' (pen) or 'phuri' (brush). Symbol of potential.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Emptiness as potential—blank paper holds infinite possibility before the first mark.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Paper carried knowledge—the precious sheet that preserved thought across time and distance.
